channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Forget Coding. The World's Hottest Jobs Now Belong To Plumbers, Electricians
item.description: As many as 85% of companies in blue-collar industries say they have unfilled vacancies - even as world's most talented coders and engineers are going unemployed.
item.content:encoded: As many as 85% of companies in blue-collar industries say they have unfilled vacancies - even as world's most talented coders and engineers are going unemployed.

item.title: US Confirms "Flesh-Eating" New World Screwworm Case In Texas
item.description: USDA officials said the Texas detection is being investigated, and control measures are underway to prevent spread.
item.content:encoded: USDA officials said the Texas detection is being investigated, and control measures are underway to prevent spread.

item.title: "US, Israel Trying To Sow Division After Decisive Blow": Mojtaba Khamenei

media:content.media:description: A woman holds a picture of Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, during a rally in Tehran

item.description: In a written message, Mojtaba Khamenei said "the malicious enemy" was seeking to "plant the seeds of doubt, despair, fear, mistrust and division" among the public.
item.content:encoded: In a written message, Mojtaba Khamenei said "the malicious enemy" was seeking to "plant the seeds of doubt, despair, fear, mistrust and division" among the public.

item.title: At Least 12 Killed In Fire At Elderly Care Home In Sri Lanka

media:content.media:description: Authorities continue to investigate the cause of the fire.

item.description: The youngest victim was a 17-year-old boy, police said.
item.content:encoded: The youngest victim was a 17-year-old boy, police said.

item.title: US-Iran Live Updates: After Battlefield Defeat, Enemies Now Seek To Sow Division In Iran: Mojtaba Khamenei

media:content.media:description: US-Iran War Live: The US said that it had "successfully defeated" a series of Iranian missile attacks.

item.description: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War Live: Iran fired missiles at Kuwait and Bahrain hours after US conducted strikes on Iran's Qeshm Island amid ongoing peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: Behind Kim Jong Un's 'Exponential' Nuclear Ramp-Up Plan Is A US Problem

media:content.media:description: Kim reportedly said the expansion was necessary given what he called "worsening security threats"

item.description: Experts point out that the conflict in the Middle East reinforced Pyongyang's long-standing view that it needs a nuclear arsenal to be able to defend itself against South Korea and the US.
item.content:encoded: Experts point out that the conflict in the Middle East reinforced Pyongyang's long-standing view that it needs a nuclear arsenal to be able to defend itself against South Korea and the US.

item.title: US Wildlife Officials Ask Residents To Help Stop Invasive 4-Foot Tegu Lizards
item.description: Residents are advised to humanely euthanise tegus if encountered and to report sightings to local wildlife departments.
item.content:encoded: Residents are advised to humanely euthanise tegus if encountered and to report sightings to local wildlife departments.

item.title: Adam Hamawy, US Doctor Who Served In Gaza Is On Path To US Congress

media:content.media:description: The army veteran was part of the medical mission to the Palestinian enclave in 2024.

item.description: Hamawy’s victory secures him the Democratic nomination for New Jersey’s 12th Congressional District, a strongly Democratic seat.
item.content:encoded: Hamawy’s victory secures him the Democratic nomination for New Jersey’s 12th Congressional District, a strongly Democratic seat.

item.title: Pollution And Energy Use To Double In 4 Years As AI Grows: UN Study
item.description: By 2030, data centers will account for nearly 3% of the world's projected electricity use, with 935 trillion watt-hours.
item.content:encoded: By 2030, data centers will account for nearly 3% of the world's projected electricity use, with 935 trillion watt-hours.

item.title: Trump Official Ben Black Named In Thousands Of Epstein Records

media:content.media:description: Ben Black currently leads the US International Development Finance Corporation

item.description: Epstein also claimed to have advised Ben on the purchase of an $11.5 million townhouse.
item.content:encoded: Epstein also claimed to have advised Ben on the purchase of an $11.5 million townhouse.

item.title: From The Sahara's Sands, A New And Dangerous Order Is Taking Shape Fast
item.description: The Malian Defence Minister has been assassinated, separatists have 'partnered' with Al Qaeda-linked groups, and a military junta, backed by Russian forces, is working overtime to douse the fires.
item.content:encoded: The Malian Defence Minister has been assassinated, separatists have 'partnered' with Al Qaeda-linked groups, and a military junta, backed by Russian forces, is working overtime to douse the fires.

item.title: Ohio University Reaches $100 Million Settlement With 280 Sex Abuse Victims
item.description: The settlement with 279 of the 280 former students was ratified by the university's board on Wednesday. It followed years of litigation over accusations of decades of abuse by Richard Strauss.
item.content:encoded: The settlement with 279 of the 280 former students was ratified by the university's board on Wednesday. It followed years of litigation over accusations of decades of abuse by Richard Strauss.

item.title: Nepali Climber Missing On Everest For 6 Days Found Alive

media:content.media:description: At least five people have died this season

item.description: The experienced Hillary Dawa Sherpa vanished on the upper reaches of the world's highest mountain early on May 30.
item.content:encoded: The experienced Hillary Dawa Sherpa vanished on the upper reaches of the world's highest mountain early on May 30.

item.title: Donald Trump Defines Iran 'Ceasefire' As "Shooting In Moderate Manner"

media:content.media:description: Trump is facing warnings from foes and allies alike that he's getting boxed in on the Iran war

item.description: The American commander-in-chief also implied that it was possible that the US and Iran could come together for a peace deal "over the weekend".
item.content:encoded: The American commander-in-chief also implied that it was possible that the US and Iran could come together for a peace deal "over the weekend".

item.title: Over 700 Whales, Dolphins Massacred In Barbaric Viking-Era Grind On Faroe Islands

media:content.media:description: Over 700 whales and dolphins were slaughtered in the Faroe Islands' annual grindadrap tradition.

item.description: Over 700 whales and dolphins were killed in the Faroe Islands grindadrap hunt, sparking animal rights backlash.
item.content:encoded: Over 700 whales and dolphins were killed in the Faroe Islands grindadrap hunt, sparking animal rights backlash.

item.title: Food Contamination Causes 1.5 Million Deaths Annually: WHO

media:content.media:description: Africa and southeast Asia alone account for nearly three-quarters of all cases

item.description: After analysing 194 countries between 2000 and 2021, the United Nations health agency found that 886 million people contract an illness linked to the consumption of unsafe food a year
item.content:encoded: After analysing 194 countries between 2000 and 2021, the United Nations health agency found that 886 million people contract an illness linked to the consumption of unsafe food a year

item.title: Visa And Mastercard Payments Suspended In Cuba Following US Sanctions

media:content.media:description: Sanctions have targeted Cuba's military conglomerate and major economic player GAESA

item.description: Washington-Havana relations have tumbled as the United States has slapped sanctions on Cuba, which has also been under a US-imposed energy blockade since January.
item.content:encoded: Washington-Havana relations have tumbled as the United States has slapped sanctions on Cuba, which has also been under a US-imposed energy blockade since January.

item.title: South Korea, China To Expand Weekly Flight Rights For First Time In 7 Years

media:content.media:description: The deal would make it easier to add flights on high-demand routes

item.description: The agreement will increase passenger flight rights by 56 weekly flights to 664 from 608, and cargo rights by 14 weekly flights to 68 from 54
item.content:encoded: The agreement will increase passenger flight rights by 56 weekly flights to 664 from 608, and cargo rights by 14 weekly flights to 68 from 54

item.title: Trump Signs Order To Ease Firing Of 8,000 High-Paid Government Workers

media:content.media:description: Trump is persisting in his efforts to fire employees who he sees as undermining his political goals

item.description: The order, released by the White House and the Office of Personnel Management, strips job protections from a mostly senior group of federal workers earning up to almost $200,000 a year
item.content:encoded: The order, released by the White House and the Office of Personnel Management, strips job protections from a mostly senior group of federal workers earning up to almost $200,000 a year

item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Opposes US Approval For AI Model Releases

media:content.media:description: Altman's visit to Washington coincides with a critical period for the company and the industry

item.description: Sam Altman will ask Congress to increase funding for artificial intelligence testing at the US Department of Commerce. The department already works with companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic to test their models.
item.content:encoded: Sam Altman will ask Congress to increase funding for artificial intelligence testing at the US Department of Commerce. The department already works with companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic to test their models.
